name: Security Policy Changes
description: Detects audit policy changes, domain policy modifications, and security descriptor changes. Event 4719 is critical — attackers frequently disable audit policies to cover their tracks. Event 4906 (CrashOnAuditFail) modification can be used to crash systems when audit log is full.
author: EventHawk (SANS/Microsoft/Sigma)

event_descriptions.4715: Audit policy on an object changed
event_descriptions.4719: System audit policy changed — SubcategoryName, AuditPolicyChanges (enabling/disabling audit subcategories). Attackers use this to blind logging.
event_descriptions.4739: Domain policy changed — password policy, lockout policy, Kerberos policy modifications
event_descriptions.4864: Namespace collision was detected — Local Policy Namespace
event_descriptions.4902: Per-user audit policy table was created
event_descriptions.4904: Attempt to register security event source
event_descriptions.4905: Attempt to unregister security event source
event_descriptions.4906: CrashOnAuditFail value changed — can be used to DoS system when audit log is full
event_descriptions.4907: Auditing settings on object changed — ObjectName, SubcategoryName
event_descriptions.1102: Audit log cleared — SubjectUserName, SubjectDomainName. Anti-forensic indicator.
event_descriptions.517: Audit log cleared (Windows XP/2003 equivalent of 1102)
